AAPL.rationale: P/E has compressed from 41x to 36.4x on 23% EPS growth driven largely by buybacks rather than revenue acceleration (only +6.4% YoY), leaving AAPL fairly-to-slightly-expensive at a multiple that still demands earnings reacceleration not yet visible in fundamentals.
key_factors: P/E at 36.4x (Dec 2025) vs 41.2x (Dec 2024) — multiple compressed 12% as EPS grew 23% to $7.46, but 36x still elevated for 6% revenue growth
FY2025 revenue $416.2B (+6.4% YoY) with flat-to-modest growth trajectory — no structural acceleration despite AI/iPhone narrative
EPS growth of 23% ($6.08 to $7.46) heavily aided by share buybacks reducing diluted shares ~12% — organic earnings growth more modest
Sentiment positive (avg +0.25 Nov-Dec 2025) with 2026 rebound narrative, but stock already up 11.5% from Jan 2025 levels, pricing in much of the optimism

Debt reduced to $54.6B from $64B peak, equity expanded to $9.6B — D/E improving from 8x

9-month EPS growth only 8.1% YoY ($11.86 vs $10.97) — Q2 2025 actually declined 1.7% YoY before Q3 rebounded
Stock +42% over past year ($261 to $370) with nearly all returns from P/E expansion, not earnings growth
Consensus bullish sentiment (Buffett halo, multiple 'top buy for 2026' lists) limits upside surprise potential

P/B at 15.1x and P/S at 4.3x are at the top of CAT's historical range — these multiples were previously only seen at absolute cycle peaks

payload.assistant_text:
View submitted. Here's the summary:

**AMZN: Score +0.50 | Conviction 0.68 | Horizon: Year**

**Core thesis:** AMZN has undergone significant P/E compression (44.6x → 32.7x) over the past 6 months while fundamentals actually improved — TTM operating income at $76.2B, operating margin ~11%, ROE 21%. The stock is flat for 2025 (+6%) while the S&P gained 17%, creating a quality compounder at a historically cheap valuation. 46 of 47 analysts are bullish with an average 12-month target of $284 (~23% upside). Q4 earnings in late January are the near-term catalyst.

**Key factors:**
- P/E diluted 32.7x vs. 5-year average 44.3x — cheapest relative to own history
- Operating margin expanded from ~2.5% to ~11% over two years (AWS + advertising)
- Near-unanimous analyst consensus with $284 avg PT (+23%)
- Stock range-bound $220-242 since July suggests accumulation before breakout

**Prior view revision:** Bumped score from +0.45 to +0.50 — the thesis has strengthened as P/E compressed further while business grew.

CRM.final_explanation: CRM remains a quality compounder with genuine margin expansion, but the 12% rally since October has re-expanded valuation to ~42x P/E, narrowing risk-reward from the more asymmetric entry at 37x.

CVX.final_explanation: CVX's valuation has disconnected from fundamentals — 23x P/E on declining earnings in a bearish oil environment with $56/bbl Brent forecasts for 2026. Prior bearish views have been validated by continued earnings deterioration; the case has strengthened with stock higher and outlook worse.
